name: apple-tv-remote
title: Apple TV Remote
description: Control your Apple TV from Raycast with a visual remote, app launcher, deep links, and AI commands — no external installs required.
author: camschroedes

commands.name: remote
commands.title: Apple TV Remote
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: On-screen remote with d-pad, playback, volume, and text input.

commands.name: ask
commands.title: Ask Apple TV
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: One-off natural-language commands: pause, open Netflix, play a show.

commands.name: setup
commands.title: Set up Apple TV
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Discover and pair with an Apple TV on your network.

commands.name: launch-app
commands.title: Launch Apple TV App
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Browse and open apps installed on your Apple TV.

commands.name: play-pause
commands.title: Play/Pause
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Toggle playback on your Apple TV.

commands.name: home
commands.title: Go to Home Screen
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Jump to the Apple TV home screen.

commands.name: menubar
commands.title: Apple TV Menu Bar
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Quick Apple TV controls from your menu bar.

commands.name: up
commands.title: Up
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Press Up on the Apple TV remote.

commands.name: down
commands.title: Down
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Press Down on the Apple TV remote.

commands.name: left
commands.title: Left
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Press Left on the Apple TV remote.

commands.name: right
commands.title: Right
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Press Right on the Apple TV remote.

commands.name: select
commands.title: Select
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Press Select on the Apple TV remote.

commands.name: back
commands.title: Back
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Go back (Menu) on the Apple TV.

commands.name: volume-up
commands.title: Volume up
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Turn the Apple TV volume up.

commands.name: volume-down
commands.title: Volume Down
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Turn the Apple TV volume down.

commands.name: skip-forward
commands.title: Skip Forward 10 Seconds
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Skip forward in the current video.

commands.name: skip-back
commands.title: Skip Back 10 Seconds
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Skip backward in the current video.

commands.name: context-menu
commands.title: Open Context Menu
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Long-press select on the Apple TV to open the context menu for the focused item.

commands.name: app-switcher
commands.title: App Switcher
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Open the Apple TV app switcher.

commands.name: control-center
commands.title: Control Center
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Open Control Center on the Apple TV.

commands.name: sleep
commands.title: Sleep Apple TV
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Put the Apple TV to sleep.

commands.name: wake
commands.title: Wake Apple TV
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Wake the Apple TV up.

commands.name: screensaver
commands.title: Start Screensaver
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Start the Apple TV screensaver (can be flaky on some models).

commands.name: type-text
commands.title: Type Text on Apple TV
commands.subtitle: Apple TV
commands.description: Send text into the focused field on the Apple TV (e.g. a search box).

tools.name: remote-key
tools.title: Press Remote Key
tools.description: Press a button on the Apple TV remote: up, down, left, right, select, menu (back), home, play_pause, volume_up, volume_down, next, previous.

tools.name: launch-app
tools.title: Launch App
tools.description: Open an app on the Apple TV by name, e.g. Netflix, YouTube, Disney+.

tools.name: play-content
tools.title: Play Content
tools.description: Open a specific show or movie on the Apple TV, optionally in a specific streaming app. Resolves the title to a deep link when possible, otherwise opens the app.

tools.name: power
tools.title: Power
tools.description: Put the Apple TV to sleep or wake it up.

ai.instructions: You control the user's Apple TV. For playback/navigation requests (pause, play, up, back, home, volume) call remote-key with the matching key ('pause' and 'play' both map to play_pause). When the user names an app, call launch-app. When the user names a show or movie, call play-content (include the app if they named one). For sleep/wake/'turn off'/'turn on' call power. Never invent bundle IDs or deep-link URLs; the tools resolve real identifiers themselves. If a request is ambiguous (which show, which app), ask a short clarifying question first. Relay play-content result messages verbatim; they state exactly what happened.

evals.input: @apple-tv-remote play Rick and Morty on Netflix

mocks.play-content: Opened Rick and Morty on Netflix

mocks.power: Apple TV is going to sleep

preferences.name: country
preferences.title: Streaming Country
preferences.description: Two-letter country code used to find where shows are streaming (e.g. US, AU, GB). Leave blank to auto-detect from your Mac's region.

preferences.name: searchAutomation
preferences.title: Search Automation
preferences.description: How far to go after typing a title into Apple TV Search. 'Open top result' stops on the title page so you pick the streaming app. Pressing Play blindly can start the wrong provider.

data.title: Open and press Play (may pick the wrong app)

preferences.name: connectTimeout
preferences.title: Connection Timeout
preferences.description: How long commands wait to reach the Apple TV (milliseconds).

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
